title: Just for You - Men - Canada.ca
content:
Health Canada and the Public Health Agency of Canada have many online resources on Men's health issues.
For a list of resources about your health and the surrounding environment, food safety and consumer product safety, consult the Consumer Information page.
Prostate cancer and non-melanoma skin cancer are the most commonly diagnosed cancer among Canadian men.
- Cancer Prostate Cancer Colorectal Cancer
Also see our Diseases and Conditions section.
- Family violence and your health Intimate partner violence against men and boys Engaging men and boys in preventing family violence
- Intimate partner violence against men and boys
- Engaging men and boys in preventing family violence
We have some great resources available to help you learn more about being more physically active and making healthier eating choices.
- Canada's Physical Activity Guide
- Eating Well with Canada's Food Guide - First Nations, Inuit and Métis
Also see our Physical Activity section.
At younger ages, men are at a much higher risk than women of developing coronary artery disease. Smoking, physical inactivity, obesity and diabetes continue to be risk factors.
- Cardiovascular Disease
- Safe Use of Health Products for Weight Loss
- Mental Health - Coping with Stress
Also see our Mental Health section.
- Laser Eye Surgery for Vision Correction
- Reducing Work-Life Conflict: What Works? What Doesn't?
Our goal is to make this page as useful as possible and your comments can help.
